[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendBNB / StabilityPool._sendBNBGainToDepositor] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor with a malicious receiver contract) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has a non-zero BNB gain in StabilityPool, attacker's address is a contract with a fallback that re-enters withdrawFromSP or provideToSP) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawFromSP -> StabilityPool._sendBNBGainToDepositor sends BNB via call{value} to attacker contract -> fallback re-enters withdrawFromSP or provideToSP before deposit snapshot is fully settled), violating INVARIANT (each depositor's BNB gain must be paid out exactly once per accounting period; ActivePool.BNB and StabilityPool.BNB must decrease by exactly the amount credited to depositors), causing scoped impact: attacker receives BNB gain more than once or claims BNB gain while simultaneously manipulating their deposit snapshot to retain future gain entitlement? Local proof idea: write a Foundry unit test deploying a MaliciousReceiver contract that re-enters withdrawFromSP in its receive() fallback; assert that attacker's total BNB received exceeds getDepositorBNBGain() computed before the first withdrawal.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.withdrawBNBGainToTrove / BorrowerOperations.moveBNBGainToTrove / ActivePool.receive] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor with an open trove) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has accumulated BNB gain in StabilityPool, attacker's trove is active) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawBNBGainToTrove -> StabilityPool decrements its BNB tracker and calls borrowerOperations.moveBNBGainToTrove{value: depositorBNBGain} -> BorrowerOperations calls _activePoolAddColl which sends ETH to ActivePool.receive() -> ActivePool.BNB increases -> attacker's trove collateral increases), violating INVARIANT (the net BNB flow must be zero: StabilityPool.BNB decreases by X, ActivePool.BNB increases by X, no BNB is created; attacker's trove collateral increase must equal exactly the BNB gain they were entitled to), causing scoped impact: attacker's trove collateral is inflated beyond their entitled BNB gain, allowing them to withdraw excess collateral or borrow more USDS than entitled? Local proof idea: write a Foundry invariant test asserting StabilityPool.BNB + ActivePool.BNB + DefaultPool.BNB == constant after any withdrawBNBGainToTrove call; fuzz depositorBNBGain values and assert trove coll delta equals gain.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.provideToSP / _triggerSABLEIssuance / _updateG] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (USDS holder) under PRECONDITIONS (CommunityIssuance has accumulated unreleased SABLE over a long time period with zero or near-zero totalUSDSDeposits, attacker holds sufficient USDS) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls provideToSP with a large USDS amount just before or at the same block as a large SABLE issuance event -> _triggerSABLEIssuance is called at the start of provideToSP, issuing SABLE proportional to time elapsed -> attacker's deposit snapshot is taken at the current G after issuance -> attacker immediately calls withdrawFromSP to collect SABLE gain), violating INVARIANT (SABLE rewards must be distributed proportionally to time-weighted deposits; a depositor who was not present during the accrual period must not receive SABLE for that period), causing scoped impact: attacker captures SABLE rewards accrued during a period when they had no deposit, extracting SABLE from CommunityIssuance without proportional USDS time-lock? Local proof idea: write a Foundry fuzz test with time.warp(N days) and zero deposits, then deposit and immediately withdraw; assert SABLE received equals only the issuance for the single block, not the entire N-day accrual.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool._payOutSABLEGains / getFrontEndSABLEGain / _updateFrontEndStakeAndSnapshots] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (registered front-end operator with kickbackRate=0) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker registers as front-end with kickbackRate=0, multiple depositors tag their deposits with attacker's front-end address, SABLE issuance accumulates over time) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawFromSP with _amount=0 or provideToSP with 1 wei -> _payOutSABLEGains pays out getFrontEndSABLEGain(attacker) which is (1-kickbackRate)*totalGain = 100% of the front-end's share -> attacker receives SABLE while depositors only receive 0% kickback), violating INVARIANT (depositors who tagged a front-end with kickbackRate=0 receive 0 SABLE; the front-end captures 100% of the SABLE earned by those deposits; this is by design but the question is whether the front-end can claim repeatedly or claim gains not yet earned), causing scoped impact: front-end operator extracts all SABLE gains from tagged depositors without any deposit of their own, effectively stealing SABLE rewards from depositors who chose a 0-kickback front-end? Local proof idea: deploy a front-end with kickbackRate=0, have victim deposit 1000 USDS tagged to it, advance time, have front-end call withdrawFromSP(0), assert front-end received all SABLE and depositor received 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool._getBNBGainFromSnapshots / _getCompoundedStakeFromSnapshots / scale boundary logic] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (currentScale is about to increment due to a near-pool-emptying liquidation, attacker has a deposit with scaleSnapshot == currentScale) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker deposits just before the scale-incrementing liquidation -> liquidation occurs, currentScale increments -> attacker's BNB gain calculation uses firstPortion = epochToScaleToSum[epoch][scaleSnapshot] - S_snapshot and secondPortion = epochToScaleToSum[epoch][scaleSnapshot+1] / SCALE_FACTOR -> attacker claims BNB gain spanning the scale boundary), violating INVARIANT (BNB gain for a deposit spanning one scale change must equal exactly d0*(S_i - S_t + S_{i+1}/1e9)/P_t; no depositor should receive more BNB than the collateral distributed during their deposit period), causing scoped impact: attacker receives excess BNB gain due to scale boundary arithmetic allowing double-counting of the S sum at the boundary epoch/scale? Local proof idea: write a differential fuzz test comparing manual BNB gain calculation against _getBNBGainFromSnapshots across scale transitions; assert gains sum to exactly the BNB distributed.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool._updateG / _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked / lastSABLEError] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker controls the timing of deposits/withdrawals to manipulate totalUSDSDeposits at the moment of SABLE issuance, lastSABLEError has accumulated a large value from prior small issuances) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker withdraws most of their deposit to reduce totalUSDSDeposits to a very small value -> SABLE issuance event occurs -> SABLENumerator = SABLEIssuance*1e18 + lastSABLEError is divided by tiny totalUSDSDeposits -> SABLEPerUnitStaked is inflated -> attacker re-deposits to capture inflated G -> attacker withdraws and claims excess SABLE), violating INVARIANT (total SABLE distributed via G must not exceed total SABLE issued by CommunityIssuance; each depositor's SABLE gain must be proportional to their time-weighted deposit share), causing scoped impact: attacker extracts more SABLE than their proportional share by manipulating the error correction accumulator and deposit size at issuance time? Local proof idea: write a Foundry fuzz test with variable totalUSDSDeposits at issuance time; assert sum of all depositor SABLE gains <= total SABLE issued.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.offset / _computeRewardsPerUnitStaked / lastBNBError_Offset] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator or anyone who can trigger liquidations) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker can trigger many small liquidations in sequence, lastBNBError_Offset accumulates across calls, totalUSDSDeposits is large) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker triggers N small liquidations each with tiny _collToAdd -> BNBNumerator = _collToAdd*1e18 + lastBNBError_Offset -> BNBGainPerUnitStaked = BNBNumerator / totalUSDSDeposits -> lastBNBError_Offset = BNBNumerator - BNBGainPerUnitStaked*totalUSDSDeposits accumulates -> after N liquidations, the accumulated error inflates S beyond the actual BNB received by StabilityPool), violating INVARIANT (sum of all depositor BNB gains computed from S must not exceed actual BNB transferred from ActivePool to StabilityPool via sendBNB), causing scoped impact: depositors collectively claim more BNB than was transferred from ActivePool, draining ActivePool.BNB below its accounting liability? Local proof idea: write an invariant test asserting StabilityPool.BNB >= sum of all getDepositorBNBGain() after N sequential small liquidations.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.offset / _triggerSABLEIssuance / provideToSP ordering] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (a liquidation is pending, attacker can front-run the liquidation transaction) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls provideToSP with large USDS amount -> _triggerSABLEIssuance is called, issuing SABLE for time elapsed -> attacker's deposit snapshot is taken at current G -> liquidation occurs calling offset -> _triggerSABLEIssuance issues SABLE again for the same block -> G is updated -> attacker's deposit now earns SABLE from the liquidation-triggered issuance despite having just deposited), violating INVARIANT (a depositor who just entered the pool in the same block as a liquidation should not earn SABLE from the issuance triggered by that liquidation; their snapshot G should equal the post-issuance G), causing scoped impact: attacker captures SABLE rewards from a liquidation-triggered issuance event by front-running the liquidation with a deposit in the same block? Local proof idea: write a Foundry test with two transactions in the same block (provideToSP then offset); assert attacker's SABLE gain from the offset issuance is zero.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._applyPendingRewards / getPendingBNBReward / _movePendingTroveRewardsToActivePool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (trove owner) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has an active trove with pending BNB rewards from redistribution, L_BNB has increased since attacker's last snapshot) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls BorrowerOperations.adjustTrove which calls troveManager.applyPendingRewards -> _applyPendingRewards computes pendingBNBReward = stake * (L_BNB - snapshotBNB) -> adds to trove coll -> calls _movePendingTroveRewardsToActivePool which calls defaultPool.sendBNBToActivePool -> ActivePool.BNB increases -> attacker then calls withdrawColl to extract the pending BNB reward as collateral), violating INVARIANT (pending BNB rewards must be applied exactly once; ActivePool.BNB increase from DefaultPool must equal exactly the pending reward credited to the trove; no double-application of the same reward snapshot), causing scoped impact: attacker double-applies pending BNB rewards by triggering applyPendingRewards multiple times before the snapshot is updated, extracting excess BNB from ActivePool? Local proof idea: write a unit test calling applyPendingRewards twice on the same trove without any intervening redistribution; assert trove coll increases only once.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.redeemCollateral / _redeemCloseTrove / CollSurplusPool.accountSurplus] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (a trove has collateral significantly above the redemption amount at current price, the trove is fully redeemed) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (redeemer calls redeemCollateral -> _redeemCollateralFromTrove computes BNBLot = USDSLot * 1e18 / price -> if newDebt == usdsGasCompensation, calls _redeemCloseTrove with _BNB = newColl = entireTroveColl - BNBLot -> collSurplusPool.accountSurplus(borrower, _BNB) -> activePool.sendBNB(collSurplusPool, _BNB) -> borrower later calls claimCollateral), violating INVARIANT (the surplus BNB credited to the borrower must equal exactly entireTroveColl - BNBLot; ActivePool.BNB must decrease by exactly BNBLot + surplus; no BNB is lost or double-counted between ActivePool and CollSurplusPool), causing scoped impact: rounding in BNBLot = USDSLot * 1e18 / price causes surplus to be over- or under-credited, allowing the borrower to claim more BNB than their entitled surplus? Local proof idea: fuzz price and USDSLot values; assert borrower_surplus + redeemer_BNB + fee == entireTroveColl for all inputs.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.redeemCollateral / activePool.sendBNB(sableStaking, BNBFee) / sableStaking.increaseF_BNB] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (SABLE/LP staker in SableStakingV2) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has staked SABLE_LP tokens in SableStakingV2, a redemption occurs routing BNB fee to sableStaking via activePool.sendBNB) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (redemption occurs -> activePool.sendBNB(sableStaking, BNBFee) -> sableStaking.increaseF_BNB(BNBFee) updates F_BNB accumulator -> attacker calls unstake or stake to trigger reward payout -> attacker receives BNB fee proportional to their stake), violating INVARIANT (BNB fee rewards in SableStakingV2 must be distributed only to stakers who were staked at the time of the fee event; a staker who enters after increaseF_BNB is called must not receive that fee; the snapshot mechanism must correctly exclude pre-entry fees), causing scoped impact: attacker front-runs a large redemption by staking just before the fee is credited to SableStakingV2, capturing BNB fee rewards they are not entitled to? Local proof idea: write a Foundry test where attacker stakes 1 block before redeemCollateral; assert attacker's BNB gain equals their proportional share only of fees accrued after their stake snapshot.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: BorrowerOperations._triggerBorrowingFee / sableStaking.increaseF_USDS / usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (SABLE/LP staker) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has staked in SableStakingV2, a borrower opens a trove or adjusts debt triggering a borrowing fee) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (borrower calls openTrove -> _triggerBorrowingFee mints USDSFee to sableStakingAddress and calls sableStaking.increaseF_USDS(USDSFee) -> F_USDS accumulator increases -> attacker calls unstake to collect USDS gain), violating INVARIANT (USDS fee rewards must be distributed only to stakers present at the time of the fee event; stakers who entered after the fee must not receive it; the per-staker snapshot of F_USDS must correctly exclude pre-entry fees), causing scoped impact: attacker front-runs a large borrowing fee event by staking just before the fee is minted to SableStakingV2, capturing USDS fee rewards they are not entitled to? Local proof idea: write a Foundry test with attacker staking 1 wei before openTrove; assert attacker's USDS gain is proportional only to fees after their snapshot.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / activePool.sendBNB(liquidator, _BNB) / _getCollGasCompensation] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker triggers a batch liquidation of N troves via batchLiquidateTroves, each trove has collGasCompensation = entireTroveColl/200) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls batchLiquidateTroves with array of N liquidatable troves -> totals.totalCollGasCompensation accumulates N * (coll_i/200) -> _sendGasCompensation calls activePool.sendBNB(attacker, totalCollGasCompensation) -> ActivePool.BNB decreases by totalCollGasCompensation), violating INVARIANT (gas compensation paid to liquidator must equal exactly sum of _getCollGasCompensation(entireTroveColl_i) for each liquidated trove; ActivePool.BNB decrease must match; no rounding error should allow liquidator to receive more than 0.5% of each trove's collateral), causing scoped impact: rounding in integer division (coll/200) across N troves creates a cumulative surplus that the liquidator captures beyond their entitled 0.5% gas compensation? Local proof idea: fuzz N trove collateral values; assert sum(coll_i/200) == totalCollGasCompensation and liquidator BNB received == totalCollGasCompensation.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool._updateRewardSumAndProduct / _getCompoundedStakeFromSnapshots / epoch reset] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (a pool-emptying liquidation occurs resetting P=1 and incrementing currentEpoch, attacker has a deposit from the previous epoch) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (pool-emptying liquidation occurs -> currentEpoch increments, P resets to 1e18 -> attacker calls withdrawFromSP -> _getCompoundedStakeFromSnapshots returns 0 because epochSnapshot < currentEpoch -> attacker's compounded deposit is 0 -> but getDepositorBNBGain still uses initialDeposit and the S values from the old epoch -> attacker claims BNB gain from the old epoch), violating INVARIANT (a depositor whose deposit was fully consumed in a pool-emptying liquidation must have zero compounded deposit AND zero BNB gain; the BNB gain must have been fully distributed during the liquidation that emptied the pool), causing scoped impact: attacker claims BNB gain from an epoch where their deposit was already fully consumed, double-counting the BNB distributed during the pool-emptying liquidation? Local proof idea: write a unit test with a pool-emptying liquidation; assert that after epoch increment, getDepositorBNBGain returns 0 for pre-epoch depositors.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.withdrawFromSP / _requireNoUnderCollateralizedTroves / _amount=0 bypass] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (there are under-collateralized troves in the system, attacker has accumulated BNB and SABLE gains) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls withdrawFromSP with _amount=0 -> the check _requireNoUnderCollateralizedTroves is skipped because _amount==0 -> _triggerSABLEIssuance is called -> _payOutSABLEGains pays out attacker's SABLE gain -> _sendBNBGainToDepositor pays out attacker's BNB gain -> attacker receives both SABLE and BNB without withdrawing USDS), violating INVARIANT (the restriction on withdrawals when under-collateralized troves exist is intended to keep USDS in the pool for liquidations; bypassing it with _amount=0 to extract BNB and SABLE gains while leaving USDS deposit intact circumvents the liquidation protection), causing scoped impact: attacker extracts BNB and SABLE rewards from StabilityPool while under-collateralized troves exist, reducing the pool's ability to absorb liquidations and gaining rewards ahead of the liquidation event? Local proof idea: write a unit test with an under-collateralized trove; call withdrawFromSP(0) and assert BNB and SABLE gains are paid out; verify this is the intended behavior or a bypass.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redistributeDebtAndColl / L_BNB / L_USDSDebt / _updateStakeAndTotalStakes] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (trove owner) under PRECONDITIONS (a redistribution event occurs increasing L_BNB and L_USDSDebt, attacker's trove has a large stake relative to totalStakes) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (redistribution occurs -> L_BNB += collToRedistribute * 1e18 / totalStakes -> attacker calls adjustTrove -> applyPendingRewards applies pendingBNBReward = stake * (L_BNB - snapshotBNB) / 1e18 -> attacker's trove coll increases -> attacker calls withdrawColl to extract the redistributed BNB), violating INVARIANT (total BNB redistributed via L_BNB must equal exactly collToRedistribute; sum of all pending BNB rewards across all active troves must not exceed DefaultPool.BNB; precision loss in L_BNB calculation must not create extractable surplus), causing scoped impact: rounding in L_BNB = L_BNB + collToRedistribute*1e18/totalStakes causes cumulative precision loss where sum of all pending rewards exceeds actual DefaultPool.BNB, allowing the last claimant to drain DefaultPool? Local proof idea: write an invariant test asserting sum(getPendingBNBReward(trove_i)) <= DefaultPool.getBNB() after any redistribution.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._liquidateRecoveryMode / TroveHelper.getCappedOffsetVals / CollSurplusPool.accountSurplus] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (trove owner whose trove is liquidated in recovery mode with ICR >= MCR) under PRECONDITIONS (system is in recovery mode, attacker's trove has MCR <= ICR < TCR and entireTroveDebt <= USDSInStabPool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (liquidation occurs -> getCappedOffsetVals computes cappedCollPortion = debt * MCR / price -> collSurplus = entireTroveColl - cappedCollPortion -> accountSurplus(borrower, collSurplus) -> activePool.sendBNB(collSurplusPool, collSurplus) -> borrower calls claimCollateral), violating INVARIANT (the surplus credited to the borrower must equal exactly entireTroveColl - cappedCollPortion; ActivePool.BNB must decrease by exactly collToSendToSP + collGasCompensation + collSurplus = entireTroveColl; no BNB is lost or double-counted), causing scoped impact: rounding in cappedCollPortion = debt * MCR / price causes collSurplus to be over-credited, allowing the borrower to claim more BNB than their entitled surplus from the recovery mode liquidation? Local proof idea: fuzz debt, MCR, price values; assert collToSendToSP + collGasCompensation + collSurplus == entireTroveColl for all inputs.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: ActivePool.sendBNB / _requireCallerIsBOorTroveMorSP / StabilityPool.offset] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker can trigger a liquidation where _collToAdd > 0 and _debtToOffset > 0) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (TroveManager calls stabilityPool.offset(debtToOffset, collToSendToSP) -> offset calls activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt(debtToOffset) then activePool.sendBNB(address(this), collToSendToSP) -> ActivePool.BNB decreases by collToSendToSP -> StabilityPool.BNB increases by collToSendToSP -> S is updated based on collToSendToSP / totalUSDSDeposits), violating INVARIANT (ActivePool.BNB + StabilityPool.BNB + DefaultPool.BNB must remain constant across any liquidation; the BNB moved from ActivePool to StabilityPool must equal exactly collToSendToSP; no BNB is created or destroyed), causing scoped impact: if collToSendToSP is computed with rounding error in _getOffsetAndRedistributionVals (collToSendToSP = coll * debtToOffset / debt), the remaining collToRedistribute may be miscalculated, causing BNB to be lost or double-counted between pools? Local proof idea: write an invariant test asserting ActivePool.BNB + StabilityPool.BNB + DefaultPool.BNB == constant before and after every liquidation.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool._getCompoundedStakeFromSnapshots / getDepositorBNBGain / initialDeposit vs compoundedDeposit] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker's deposit has been nearly fully consumed by liquidations such that compoundedDeposit < initialDeposit/1e9, causing _getCompoundedStakeFromSnapshots to return 0) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker's compounded deposit rounds to 0 -> attacker calls withdrawFromSP -> getDepositorBNBGain uses initialDeposit (not compoundedDeposit) in the formula d0*(S-S_t)/P_t -> attacker receives BNB gain computed on the full initialDeposit -> attacker's deposit is set to 0 -> attacker has received BNB gain as if their full deposit was present), violating INVARIANT (BNB gain must be computed on the actual compounded deposit, not the initial deposit; if compounded deposit rounds to 0, BNB gain should also be 0 or negligible; the BNB gain formula using initialDeposit may overstate gains when compounded deposit is near-zero), causing scoped impact: attacker receives excess BNB gain because the gain formula uses initialDeposit while the deposit accounting uses compoundedDeposit, creating a discrepancy that extracts BNB from StabilityPool? Local proof idea: write a unit test where a series of liquidations reduces compoundedDeposit to < initialDeposit/1e9; assert BNB gain received equals the mathematically correct value based on actual deposit.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: BorrowerOperations.closeTrove / activePool.sendBNB(msg.sender, coll) / TroveManager.applyPendingRewards] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (trove owner) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker's trove has pending BNB rewards from redistribution that have not been applied, attacker has sufficient USDS to repay debt) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls closeTrove -> applyPendingRewards is called, adding pendingBNBReward to trove coll -> trove coll now includes pending rewards -> _repayUSDS burns debt -> activePool.sendBNB(msg.sender, coll) sends the full coll including pending rewards to attacker), violating INVARIANT (when a trove is closed, the BNB sent to the owner must equal the trove's collateral including pending rewards; DefaultPool.BNB must decrease by pendingBNBReward; ActivePool.BNB must first increase by pendingBNBReward then decrease by total coll; net effect: attacker receives their entitled collateral plus pending rewards), causing scoped impact: verify that the pending reward application in closeTrove correctly moves BNB from DefaultPool to ActivePool before sending to owner; if _movePendingTroveRewardsToActivePool is not called or called with wrong amounts, attacker could receive more or less than entitled? Local proof idea: write a unit test with pending rewards; assert coll received == trove.coll + pendingBNBReward and DefaultPool.BNB decreases by pendingBNBReward.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.provideToSP / _setFrontEndTag / deposits[msg.sender].frontEndTag] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (StabilityPool depositor) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has an existing deposit tagged with front-end A, attacker wants to switch to front-end B or zero address to capture more SABLE) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls provideToSP with _frontEndTag=B -> since initialDeposit != 0, _setFrontEndTag is NOT called (only called when initialDeposit==0) -> attacker's deposit remains tagged with front-end A -> attacker's SABLE gain is split with front-end A at the old kickback rate -> attacker cannot change their front-end tag without fully withdrawing first), violating INVARIANT (this is by design, but the question is whether an attacker can exploit the tag-change restriction: if attacker fully withdraws to reset tag, then re-deposits with a different front-end, do they lose any SABLE gains or BNB gains in the process?), causing scoped impact: attacker loses SABLE gains by being forced to withdraw and re-deposit to change front-end tag, or conversely, can attacker exploit the tag-change to claim gains under one front-end and then switch to capture gains under another? Local proof idea: write a state-transition test: deposit with FE-A, accumulate gains, withdraw all, re-deposit with FE-B; assert no SABLE gains are lost or double-counted.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager.redeemCollateral / _updateBaseRateFromRedemption / baseRate decay] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (baseRate has decayed to near-zero, attacker holds large USDS balance, BOOTSTRAP_PERIOD=0 days) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker calls redeemCollateral with large _USDSamount -> _updateBaseRateFromRedemption increases baseRate based on totalBNBDrawn/totalUSDSSupply -> BNBFee = totalBNBDrawn * (baseRate + REDEMPTION_FEE_FLOOR) -> fee is sent to sableStaking via activePool.sendBNB -> attacker receives BNBToSendToRedeemer = totalBNBDrawn - BNBFee), violating INVARIANT (the redemption fee must be correctly computed and routed to sableStaking; ActivePool.BNB must decrease by exactly totalBNBDrawn; sableStaking must receive exactly BNBFee; redeemer must receive exactly BNBToSendToRedeemer; no BNB is lost), causing scoped impact: if baseRate decay and update arithmetic has rounding errors, the fee sent to sableStaking via activePool.sendBNB may differ from increaseF_BNB amount, creating an accounting mismatch that allows stakers to claim more BNB than was actually received? Local proof idea: fuzz totalBNBDrawn and baseRate; assert BNBFee + BNBToSendToRedeemer == totalBNBDrawn and sableStaking.BNB increases by exactly BNBFee.

[File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: ActivePool.receive / _requireCallerIsBorrowerOperationsOrDefaultPool / forced ETH] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker can send ETH to ActivePool via selfdestruct or coinbase, bypassing the receive() guard) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker selfdestructs a contract with ETH balance targeting ActivePool -> ActivePool's raw ETH balance increases but ActivePool.BNB state variable does NOT increase -> subsequent calls to sendBNB can send up to ActivePool.BNB worth of ETH -> the extra ETH is stranded), violating INVARIANT (ActivePool.BNB must equal the actual ETH balance minus any forced ETH; forced ETH must not be extractable by any caller; sendBNB must not allow sending more than ActivePool.BNB), causing scoped impact: forced ETH in ActivePool cannot be extracted by an unprivileged attacker (sendBNB is access-controlled), but could a privileged caller (TroveManager, BorrowerOperations, StabilityPool) inadvertently route the forced ETH to an attacker by sending more than the accounting allows? [File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: StabilityPool.offset / _moveOffsetCollAndDebt / activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt then activePool.sendBNB] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (a liquidation is triggered, _collToAdd and _debtToOffset are computed) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (TroveManager calls stabilityPool.offset -> _moveOffsetCollAndDebt calls activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt(_debtToOffset) -> then usdsToken.burn(address(this), _debtToOffset) -> then activePool.sendBNB(address(this), _collToAdd) -> StabilityPool.BNB increases), violating INVARIANT (the ordering of decreaseUSDSDebt, burn, and sendBNB must be atomic; if any step fails, the entire offset must revert; ActivePool.USDSDebt must decrease by exactly _debtToOffset; ActivePool.BNB must decrease by exactly _collToAdd; StabilityPool.BNB must increase by exactly _collToAdd), causing scoped impact: if activePool.sendBNB reverts after decreaseUSDSDebt has already been called, the USDSDebt is reduced without the corresponding BNB transfer, creating a permanent accounting mismatch that allows future over-withdrawal of BNB from ActivePool? [File: 'File Name: src/ActivePool.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: TroveManager._redistributeDebtAndColl / _updateSystemSnapshots_excludeCollRemainder / totalStakesSnapshot] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (trove owner) under PRECONDITIONS (a liquidation occurs updating totalStakesSnapshot and totalCollateralSnapshot, attacker's trove has a large stake) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (liquidation occurs -> _updateSystemSnapshots_excludeCollRemainder sets totalStakesSnapshot = totalStakes and totalCollateralSnapshot = activePool.getBNB + defaultPool.getBNB - collGasCompensation -> L_BNB += collToRedistribute * 1e18 / totalStakesSnapshot -> attacker calls adjustTrove -> applyPendingRewards computes pendingBNBReward = stake * (L_BNB - snapshotBNB) / 1e18), violating INVARIANT (the stake used in getPendingBNBReward must be the stake at the time of the snapshot, not the current stake; if stake changes between redistribution and reward application, the reward must still be computed on the snapshot stake), causing scoped impact: attacker increases their stake (by adding collateral) between a redistribution event and applying pending rewards, causing their pending reward to be computed on a larger stake than they had during the redistribution, extracting more BNB from DefaultPool than entitled?
